title: Thlaspi amani
taxonomy: Thlaspi amani is a species within the genus Thlaspi, which is part of the Brassicaceae family. This family includes a wide variety of flowering plants commonly known as mustards, cabbages, and allies. The Brassicaceae family is further grouped into the order Brassicales. The order Brassicales is classified under the subclass Dilleniidae and the class Magnoliopsida. The subclass Dilleniidae is part of the clade Angiosperms, which consists of flowering plants.

intro: Thlaspi amani, also known as Aman's pennycress, is a rare annual plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers and oval-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Thlaspi amani include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Thlaspi amani is primarily used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, often in combination with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
